reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to provide a plan first to get approval. In the plan, we will describe modifications:

We need to ensure /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf contains "IgnoreRhosts yes" (maybe file path from description). But we need to ensure not duplicate, no comment. Use read to check. Then use sed -i to remove any existing lines that match IgnoreRhosts; then add new line at end ensuring newline.

After editing, run sshd -t to verify syntax. Then reload sshd.

We must avoid repeating mkdir -p command. Instead we can create file with write_file if not exists. But check existence: Use read_file with path; if fails, then we need to create empty file. So we can prepare file path: /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf. Use read_file; if file not found, then write_file to create (empty). Ensure permissions 0600. Then use sed to delete old lines; add new line at end. After addition ensure newline. Then run sshd -t. Then systemctl reload sshd. These comply with rules.

We need to stop to invoke review_plan. Provide plan description.
